text: SitRep56 Tableau 7: 780 in isolation at end of day (238 confirmed / 542 suspected) at 100.3% global bed occupancy; Nord-Kivu at 160.0% saturation, Ituri 89.9%, Sud-Kivu 42.2%. Exits over 24h (62 total): 10 deaths (all Ituri), 41 non-cases, 5 recoveries (Ituri 4, NK 1), 4 escapes (all Ituri), 2 transfers to HGR; 89 admissions across the three provinces.

text: Recovered advanced to 300 DRC cumulative (+5). J-1 bed count is 755; the Tableau-7 end-of-day census is 780 (238 + 542).

text: 38 new confirmed cases and 23 deaths (73.9% community) were reported for 2026-07-09, in Ituri (32 cases, 18 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (6 cases, 5 deaths). Confirmed cases rose to 1830 and deaths to 648; the global CFR is 35.4%. The headline is flagged 'Donnees reactualisees' (DHIS2 base cleaning/harmonization).

text: No newly affected health zone this cycle; affected zones hold at 37/104. The evolving situation in Tshopo and Haut-Uele was discussed at ministerial level; those provinces remain outside the three officially affected provinces and are EXCLUDED from totals.

text: The provider strike continues in Bunia and Rwampara (Ituri). Nord-Kivu CTE occupancy is 160% and global bed occupancy is 100.3% (780 in isolation, 238 confirmed / 542 suspected).

text: Ituri concentrates 90.1% of cases (n=1663) and 85.3% of deaths (n=553), CFR 33.3%. Bunia (495), Rwampara (373), Mongbwalu (327), Nizi (107) and Nyankunde (97) remain the most affected zones; the epidemic is currently progressing fastest in Nizi.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Nord-Kivu totals 164 confirmed / 94 deaths, CFR 57.3%, carried by Katwa (68.2%), Beni (66.7%) and Butembo (46.5%); Katwa (66), Butembo (43) and Beni (30) lead notification. Vuhovi shows a 100% CFR on a very small count.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Sud-Kivu has passed 42 consecutive days without a new confirmed case (last case 2026-05-26; milestone reached 2026-07-07), opening the residual-surveillance phase toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration; 19 suspects remain in isolation and 2 samples are under analysis.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: reticence_post_mortem - Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities. - Response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: case_load_and_capacity - Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation at 160.0%; national isolation occupancy is 100.3% (780 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk. - Response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: contact_followup_gap - Contact follow-up reached 78.1% versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted. - Response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: weak_alert_reporting - Weak alert investigation in Ituri (62.1% investigation rate) risks undetected, silent transmission. - Response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: unlocalized_confirmed_cases - Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction. - Response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: essential_medicines_gap - Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management. - Response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: ipc_and_hcw_infections - Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk among health workers (112 HCW cases, 35 deaths, CFR 31.2%). - Response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: lab_backlog - A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 56.3% provincial CFR. - Response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: ambulance_isolation_gap - Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk. - Response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: coordination_gap - Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions. - Response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: financial_resource_gap - A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars. - Response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: insecurity_access - Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones. - Response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: essential_services_continuity - Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality. - Response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: population_mobility - High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; two Kisangani (Tshopo) detections (one linked to Nia-Nia) are under investigation and 159912 travelers passed through PoE/PoC this cycle. - Response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: nord_kivu_lab_logistics - Nord-Kivu reports a laboratory backlog / delayed diagnosis (5.6% positivity) and, in its logistics subset, no functional ambulance or hearse; Mutwanga and Kasindi-Lubiriha flag insufficient IPC inputs and swabs for community-death alerts. - Response: Clear the laboratory backlog, restore transport/EDS logistics, and resupply IPC inputs and swabs to the affected Nord-Kivu health zones.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Source review / Before zone-level attribution use: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones, and validate the Kisangani/Tshopo and Haut-Uele detections (one linked to Nia-Nia) before any count-bearing use.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Case management / Urgent: Relieve isolation load at 100.3% national occupancy and Nord-Kivu 160% saturation (57.3% provincial CFR); close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers and accelerate CTE construction/expansion (Bunia ETC expanding 60 to 80 beds).
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Surveillance / Ongoing: Improve contact follow-up (78.1% vs 95% target), raise Ituri alert investigation above 62.1%, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog / delayed-diagnosis and the reported lack of a functional ambulance/hearse.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Sud-Kivu transition / Residual-surveillance phase: Sustain residual epidemiological surveillance in Sud-Kivu (42+ days with no new confirmed case as of 2026-07-07) toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration, while resolving the 19 suspects still in isolation and 2 samples under analysis.
